titleStmt.title: A Transcription of the Latin text of John in Codex Sangallensis 48 (bilingual)
VL27
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Mc Lc Jo

decoDesc.p: Black ink with red and yellow decoration.

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers pages 316-395. Only the Latin is transcribed here; the Greek is available at . The Latin is written above the Greek text. It includes several alternative renderings: on some occasions where two Latin alternatives are given for one Greek word (usually separated by 'uel'), only the ending is given for the second word: the rest of the word is supplied as for an abbreviation. This manuscript also contains a number of glosses: some supply missing words, others make grammatical observations on the Greek. The Gospel begins with the Greek kephalaia, which have been partially translated, and these titles are also found in the text: both are included in the transcription.

The transcription includes foliation, lineation and all punctuation. Abbreviations have been recorded (and expanded in brackets) except the symbols for 'est', 'uel', 'autem' and 'enim'. 'u' is sometimes added above the line, possibly by the first hand. This has been rendered as (u). The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Change to verse division of 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: i(d est) muliere
The title of Chapter 10 is not translated: it should be 'de caeco nato'.
The full title of Chapter 12 is not translated.
The title of Chapter 17 is not translated: it should be 'de paracleto'.
Page 317 is blank.
Possible correction to 'leuuitas'.
The end of this word is not clear.
The first words of this verse seem to have been added later.
sic
This has been expanded as d(icit) following convention, even though the Greek has 'eipen'.
The Greek word has been erased, but the Latin retained.
A corrector appears to have put an 'i' above the first 'e'.
'uel' seems to have been omitted.
i(d est) nost(er)
There appears to be an erasure after 'nasci'.
The alternative term is not present.
The first hand has written 'uenit'. There is a dot under the 't' with 'et' above it, but 'ueni' has been written above the entire word.
'uel' has been omitted.
'uel' has been omitted.
'uel' has been omitted.
i(d est) p(ro)
'uel futurus erat' has been added by a later hand.
It is not clear whether this device of three dots has anything to do with the Latin text.
'u' added above word, presumably by first hand.
'enim', 'declinauit' and 'constituta' are in a different hand.
sic
'uel' has been omitted.
'uel' is not visible on the images.
The words 'quae superfuerunt his qui manducauerunt' are written in a different hand.
J-VL-027-B4K6V22 Five words in Greek are not translated
sic
sic
'meam' precedes 'carnem', unlike the Greek below, and there is a mark above 'mou' connecting it with 'meam'.
One Greek word is left untranslated.
The letter 'a' has been written above the letter 'a' of 'uolebant'.
A symbol connecting 'super' with the preposition of the Greek compound verb has been inserted, but the preposition has also been added in Greek in this position after the verb.
The rest of the page is blank. The Greek text of John 8:12 on this page has been deleted, but not the Latin.
The first three lines of page 349 are blank.
'de uel' is in lighter ink.
Possible correction from 'hoc'.
'uel' has been omitted.
'ueni' appears to be in lighter ink.
sic
sic
parti(cipialis)
The repetition in the Latin matches the Greek.
Alternatively, this may be expanded as 'cum uel quando diu'.
Rettig omits this correction
Rettig omits uel adpetat
i(d est) geni(tiuus)
i(d est) par(ticipialis)
'pater' is omitted, although present in Greek.
i(d est) de
The 'i' of 'gloria' is shaped in a peculiar fashion.
The first 'd' may have been deleted.
John 11:35 is omitted in Greek and Latin.
The Greek 'simonos' is left untranslated.
Rettig's reconstruction has been followed for the erased word, which corresponds to the likely reading in Greek.
There is also a correction in the Greek.
i(d est) eum
i(d est) iohannes
One Greek word is left untranslated.
One Greek word is left untranslated.
i(d est) tinctum
Two Greek words are left untranslated.
i(d est) ea
One Greek word is not translated.
One Greek word is left untranslated.
The repetition is deleted in Greek but not in Latin.
i(d est) s(ed) ami(cos)
Possible correction from arguit.
It is possible that the later hand has added 'dixerim', as an alternative literal translation of the Greek, but the addition of the Vulgate form is more likely.
The translator began by writing the φ above the Greek φ.
'habes' is written on the line above, 'uel' is omitted.
i(d est) p(ro) fut(ur)o
notam locum
The words 'notam locum' are added in the right margin.
One Greek word (khamai) is left untranslated.
i(d est) caiphę
i(d est) io(hannes)
'uel kalefiebat' added later in right margin.
'uel testare' added later.
'ekraugasan' is written in Greek.
The scribe has copied the Greek form of the name, including the ρ.
The rest of John 19:17-35 is missing in Greek and Latin.
i(d est) leg(i)s
'uel' is omitted and the letters 're' are written above the 'a' of 'impleatur'.
i(d est) sach(aria)
i(d est) io(hannes)
There is the word 'deest' in the right margin.
There is the word 'deest' in the right margin.
One Greek word (kai) is left untranslated.
deest
deest
The sign which appears either side of 'miserunt' appears to be an abbreviation for 'deest'.
There is a dash above 'illud' which may be an alternative 'illum'.
deest b(ene)d(i)x(it)
'deest benedixit' is written in the right margin.
The Greek reads Ιο-{[lig]nu{[/lig]Α.
There is an erasure behind 'quis est'.
Subscription in Greek: 'euaggelion kata iwannhn'.

The colophon is as follows: [Greek]grammata graiugenwn kata skhmata sofe gynoskis[/Greek] cerne labore meo lingua pelasga pate quisque uelit sapiens sapientes gnoscere causas | sensibus in sacris sentiat artis opes | {A new hand appears to take over here} has euangelicas doctrinas doctor ab astris | nobis pręcipiens contulit ore deus | qui regit omne quod est qui condidit omne creatum | qui factor mundi uirgine factus homo est | principium sine principio finis sine fine est | omnipotens dominus lucis origo deus | lux aeterna dei uerbum sapientia patris | caelestis patriae uerba superna dare | uenit in hunc mundum rex regum rexque sabaoth | ipse deus hominum qui dedit omne bonum | fons bonitate potens fons uitae fonsque sophiae | cor mundum famulis da bone christe tuis | saluator mundi qui sęcula sordida mundas | cum uirtute crucis crimina cuncta lauans | omnipotens miserere mei miserere dolentis | sum mihi namque mei causa doloris ego | da dolor absistat da uitam daque sophiam | des mihi perpetui te rogo regna poli.
